title: eriogonum atrorubens
intro: Eriogonum atrorubens, commonly known as the red-stemmed buckwheat, is a perennial wildflower native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The medicinal properties of Eriogonum atrorubens include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of using Eriogonum atrorubens include its ability to reduce fever, soothe wounds, and alleviate digestive problems. Eriogonum atrorubens can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties.
